minutes_briefs.title: BC firearm violation ticket rules updated to match new Firearm Violence Prevention Act
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has updated its Violation Ticket Administration and Fines Regulation to replace references to the old Firearm Act with the new Firearm Violence Prevention Act. This means that starting October 1, 2026, tickets for firearm-related offences will be issued under the new law instead of the old one. The change also removes certain outdated offences, such as operating an armoured vehicle without a permit, and adds updated rules for the same type of violation under the new act.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action is required, but review your compliance procedures for firearm-related offences to ensure they reference the new Firearm Violence Prevention Act by October 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Firearm Violence Prevention Act violation ticket rules take effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 67/2026
Made under: Offence Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: April 27, 2026

B.C. Reg. 67/2026, deposited April 27, 2026, under theOFFENCE ACT[section 132]. Order in Council 160/2026, approved and ordered April 27, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with
         the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective October 1, 2026,
         the Violation Ticket Administration and Fines Regulation, B.C. Reg. 89/97, is amended
         as set out in the attached Schedule. — N. KRIEGER,Minister of Public Safety and Solicitor General; N. SHARMA,Attorney General and Deputy Premier; J. WHITESIDE,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1Schedule 1 of the Violation Ticket Administration and Fines Regulation, B.C. Reg. 89/97,
         is amended (a)	in Column 2 of Item 1B by repealing paragraph (c), (b)	in Column 3 of Item 1B by striking out "Section 3 of theFirearm Act", (c)	in Column 2 of Item 7B by repealing paragraph (a) and substituting the following: (a)Firearm Violence Prevention Act, (d)	in Column 3 of Item 7B by adding "Every provision of theFirearm Violence Prevention Act, the contravention of which constitutes an offence, other than those provisions
            referred to in Column 1 of Schedule 2" opposite paragraph (a) in Column 2, (e)	in Column 2 of Item 9B by repealing paragraph (b) and substituting the following: (b)Firearm Violence Prevention Act, and (f)	in Column 3 of Item 9B by striking out"Section 3 of theFirearm Act" opposite paragraph (b) in Column 2 and substituting "Every provision of theFirearm Violence Prevention Act, the contravention of which constitutes an offence, other than those provisions
            referred to in Column 1 of Schedule 2". (a)	by striking out the following items as indicated: Armoured Vehicle and After-Market Compartment Control Act Operate armoured vehicle without having valid armoured vehicle permit Fail to report the installation of an after-market 	compartment in a vehicle Possess body armour without valid body armour permit (b)	by adding the following items as indicated: Armoured Vehicle and After-Market Compartment Control Act Operate armoured vehicle without permit
